relevant_files.rationale: Demonstrates AskUserQuestion pattern, evidence-based assessment, CLI state API usage (ccw learn:update-state), and robust key-based answer access. Shows how to avoid direct file writes.
Shows agent invocation pattern (learn-planning-agent via ccw cli), session creation, JIT assessment triggers, validation gates (schema → DAG → profile), and plan.json generation workflow.
Real LLM agent template showing JSON-only output constraint, MCP tool integration (ACE, Exa, smart_search), and schema validation requirements. Model for potential learn-execution-agent.
Isolated code execution engine for assessment validation. Uses Node.js permission model, sandboxing, and returns structured JSON results. Core infrastructure for learn:execute assessment validation.
Multi-layer validation pattern (schema → DAG → profile warnings). Shows how to validate plan integrity before execution. Model for execution state validation.

TypeScript CLI state API implementation showing atomic file operations (withLearnLock), schema validation (Ajv), backup/rollback, and JSON state management. Backend for learn:read-state and learn:update-state commands.
Global learn workflow state tracking active_profile_id and active_session_id. Shows state structure and metadata pattern.
Example profile structure with evidence-based topic proficiency, confidence scores, and feedback journal. Shows data model for profile updates after learning.
Plan schema defining knowledge_points structure with status enum (pending, in_progress, completed, skipped, optional), assessment types, and dependency_graph. Foundation for execution state tracking.
Comprehensive execution orchestration pattern showing TodoWrite integration, lazy loading, agent coordination, and autonomous completion. Model for learn:execute workflow structure.
Task execution pattern with agent selection logic, pre/post execution protocols, and error handling. Simpler model than workflow:execute but shows core execution concepts.
Shows CLI tool invocation pattern (ccw cli --tool codex/gemini --mode write) with background execution and timeout handling. Alternative to Task() agent calls.

agent_invocation.pattern: Real CLI Agent via ccw cli
agent_invocation.description: learn:profile and learn:plan use real LLM agents via 'ccw cli -p' with structured prompts, not simulated agents
examples: learn:plan → ccw cli with learn-planning-agent.md template
key_characteristics: Agent templates in .claude/agents/ define role and constraints
Invoked via Bash('ccw cli -p "..." --tool gemini --mode analysis/write')
Output parsed via lastJsonObjectFromText() helper
Retry + fallback chain (gemini → qwen → codex)
Background execution with run_in_background: true

state_management.pattern: CLI State API + Atomic File Operations
state_management.description: Avoid direct file writes; use CLI state API (ccw learn:read-state, ccw learn:update-state) for state.json, direct writes only for session-scoped files
examples: learn.ts: atomicWriteJson() with lock, backup, schema validation
key_characteristics: Global state (.workflow/learn/state.json) via CLI API only
Session-scoped files (sessions/*/plan.json) via direct Write()
Atomic operations: write to .tmp → validate → rename
Lock mechanism (withLearnLock) prevents concurrent corruption
Backup before write, rollback on failure
Schema validation (Ajv) before commit

user_interaction.description: Robust pattern using explicit keys for answer access, avoiding brittle Object.values()[0]
examples: const KEY = 'question_key'; const answer = AskUserQuestion({questions: [{key: KEY, ...}]}); const choice = answer[KEY];
profile.md: const SELF_ASSESS_KEY = `self_assess_${topicId}`; const answer = AskUserQuestion(...); const level = answer[SELF_ASSESS_KEY];
key_characteristics: Always define explicit key constant before AskUserQuestion
Access answer via answer[KEY], never Object.values()[0]
Supports multiSelect: false (single choice) and multiSelect: true (array)
Options array with {value, label, description} structure

validation_gates.description: Validate data through multiple layers before committing: schema → domain logic → warnings
examples: plan.md: schema validation → DAG cycle check → profile mismatch warnings
key_characteristics: Layer 0: JSON schema validation (Ajv)
Layer 1: Domain-specific validation (DAG acyclic, edge consistency)
Layer 2: Cross-entity warnings (profile-plan mismatch, high proficiency topics)
Fail fast on critical errors, collect warnings for user review
Validation output: {ok: boolean, layer0: {...}, layer1: {...}, layer2: {...}}

assessment_execution.description: Execute user code against test fixtures in isolated Node.js process with permission model
key_characteristics: Isolated temp directory per execution
Node.js permission model restricts file system access
Timeout enforcement (default 2000ms)
Structured JSON output: {tests_passed, tests_total, score, execution_time_ms, failures}
Graceful termination (SIGTERM → SIGKILL escalation)
Cleanup temp directory after execution

session_management.description: Each learning session has isolated directory with plan, interactions, and progress tracking
key_characteristics: Session ID format: LS-YYYYMMDD-NNN (e.g., LS-20250124-001)
Plan stored in sessions/${sessionId}/plan.json
Interactions tracked in sessions/${sessionId}/interactions/
JIT assessments logged in interactions/jit-assessments.json
Session folder created early (mkdir -p) before plan generation

key_findings: learn:profile uses AskUserQuestion for interactive assessment with evidence-based scoring (conceptual checks + micro-challenges via mcp-runner.js)
learn:plan invokes real LLM agent (learn-planning-agent) via 'ccw cli' to generate knowledge point DAG with resources and assessments
State management follows CLI API pattern: global state via 'ccw learn:update-state', session files via direct Write()
Atomic file operations with lock (withLearnLock), backup, schema validation (Ajv), and rollback on failure
Multi-layer validation: schema (Ajv) → domain logic (DAG cycle check) → warnings (profile-plan mismatch)
Assessment execution uses isolated sandbox (mcp-runner.js) with Node.js permission model and timeout enforcement
Agent invocation pattern: Bash('ccw cli -p "..." --tool gemini --mode analysis/write') with retry fallback chain
Output parsing: lastJsonObjectFromText() scans backwards for last valid JSON in command output
JIT assessment triggers: progressive profiling with confidence threshold (0.6), only when needed, brief (2-3 questions)
Knowledge point status enum: pending, in_progress, completed, skipped, optional (from learn-plan.schema.json)
Resource quality tiers: gold (must have ≥1 per KP), silver, bronze with quality_score (0-1)
Dependency graph validation: nodes must match KP IDs, edges must reference valid nodes, no cycles allowed
Profile update pattern: read profile → modify known_topics → update via CLI API (avoid direct file writes)
Evidence-based proficiency: each topic has proficiency (0-1), confidence (0-1), and evidence array with timestamps

separation_of_concerns.command_layer: Markdown command files (.claude/commands/learn/*.md) orchestrate workflow, handle user interaction, invoke agents
separation_of_concerns.agent_layer: Agent templates (.claude/agents/*.md) define specialized LLM roles with constraints and output schemas
separation_of_concerns.tool_layer: Internal tools (.claude/commands/learn/_internal/*.js) provide validation, execution, and utility functions
separation_of_concerns.state_layer: CLI API (ccw/src/commands/learn.ts) manages atomic state operations with locking and validation

data_flow.plan_generation: goal + profile → gap analysis → agent (learn-planning-agent) → plan.json → validation gates → session creation
data_flow.execution_flow: plan.json → select KP → present resources → user learning → assessment → update KP status → update profile → next KP

integration_points.profile_to_plan: plan.md reads active profile via 'ccw learn:read-state' → loads profile.json → passes to agent for personalization
integration_points.plan_to_execute: execute.md reads plan.json → iterates knowledge_points → tracks status (pending → in_progress → completed)
integration_points.execute_to_profile: after KP completion, update profile.known_topics with new evidence and proficiency scores

design_questions: Should learn:execute manage entire session (all KPs) or single KP execution? Recommendation: Single KP for granular control, with loop in command for session management.
How to handle resource presentation? Options: (A) Display in terminal with links, (B) Open in browser via Bash('open URL'), (C) Generate HTML summary. Recommendation: (A) for simplicity.
Assessment validation strategy? Options: (A) Mandatory for all KPs, (B) Optional with --skip-assessment flag, (C) Adaptive based on KP difficulty. Recommendation: (B) for flexibility.
Profile update timing? Options: (A) After each KP completion, (B) Batch update at session end, (C) User-triggered via separate command. Recommendation: (A) for real-time feedback.
Session resumption? Should learn:execute support --resume flag to continue interrupted sessions? Recommendation: Yes, check state.json for active_session_id.
Progress tracking? Should use TodoWrite for KP execution progress? Recommendation: Yes, following workflow:execute pattern for consistency.

Agent vs Direct Implementation? Options: (A) Create learn-execution-agent for resource curation and assessment generation, (B) Direct implementation in command. Recommendation: (B) for simplicity, agent only if complex logic needed.
CLI tool integration? Should invoke external tools (ccw cli --tool gemini) for assessment generation or use built-in logic? Recommendation: Built-in for speed, CLI for complex cases.
Error handling? How to handle assessment failures, resource unavailability, or user abandonment? Recommendation: Mark KP as 'skipped', allow retry, log to interactions/errors.json.
Feedback collection? Should collect user feedback after each KP (difficulty rating, resource quality)? Recommendation: Yes, store in profile.feedback_journal for plan improvement.

command_structure.name: learn:execute
command_structure.description: Execute learning session by presenting resources, facilitating learning, and validating knowledge acquisition

execution_phases: Phase 1: Session Discovery - Load active session from state.json or use --session-id parameter
Phase 2: Knowledge Point Selection - If --kp specified, load that KP; else find next pending/in_progress KP from plan.json
Phase 3: Resource Presentation - Display resources with quality indicators, open links if requested
Phase 4: Learning Facilitation - User studies resources, command waits or provides interactive notes
Phase 5: Assessment Validation - Run assessment (practical_task, code_challenge, or multiple_choice) via mcp-runner.js
Phase 6: Profile Update - Update known_topics with new evidence, proficiency, and confidence scores
Phase 7: Progress Tracking - Update KP status in plan.json, advance to next KP or complete session

state_management_strategy.global_state: Use 'ccw learn:read-state' and 'ccw learn:update-state' for state.json (active_session_id)
state_management_strategy.session_state: Direct Read/Write for sessions/${sessionId}/plan.json and interactions/*
state_management_strategy.profile_updates: Read profile.json → modify → Write back (no CLI API for profile updates yet)
state_management_strategy.atomic_operations: Follow atomicWriteJson pattern: write to .tmp → validate → rename

assessment_execution_strategy.practical_task: Present task description, user implements, validate via mcp-runner.js with fixture
assessment_execution_strategy.code_challenge: Similar to practical_task but with stricter acceptance criteria
assessment_execution_strategy.multiple_choice: Use AskUserQuestion with options, validate answer against correct_answer in assessment

progress_tracking_strategy.todowrite_integration: Generate TodoWrite with all KPs from plan.json, update status as execution progresses
progress_tracking_strategy.status_transitions: pending → in_progress (on start) → completed (on pass) / skipped (on skip) / optional (user choice)
progress_tracking_strategy.checkpoint_updates: After each KP completion, update plan.json and TodoWrite

error_handling_strategy.assessment_failure: Allow retry (max 3 attempts), offer skip option, log failures to interactions/errors.json
error_handling_strategy.resource_unavailable: Mark resource as unavailable, suggest alternatives, continue with remaining resources
error_handling_strategy.session_interruption: Save progress to plan.json, allow --resume to continue from last in_progress KP
error_handling_strategy.validation_errors: Catch schema validation errors, rollback to backup, log error details

lastJsonObjectFromText.purpose: Parse JSON from command output by scanning backwards for last valid JSON object

withLearnLock.purpose: Acquire exclusive lock for atomic state operations

atomicWriteJson.purpose: Write JSON with backup, validation, and rollback on failure

AskUserQuestion_pattern.purpose: Robust key-based answer access for user questions
AskUserQuestion_pattern.usage: const KEY = 'question_key'; const answer = AskUserQuestion({questions: [{key: KEY, ...}]}); const choice = answer[KEY];

mcp_runner_invocation.purpose: Execute code assessment in isolated sandbox
mcp_runner_invocation.usage: const raw = Bash('node .claude/commands/learn/_internal/mcp-runner.js ${codePath} ${fixture} --timeout-ms=2000'); const result = lastJsonObjectFromText(raw);

validation_pipeline.purpose: Multi-layer validation with schema, domain logic, and warnings

next_steps: Create learn:execute.md command file following workflow:execute.md structure
Define execution phases: discovery → selection → presentation → learning → assessment → update → progress
Implement TodoWrite integration for KP progress tracking
Add assessment execution logic using mcp-runner.js for code challenges
Implement profile update logic after KP completion (evidence-based proficiency updates)
Add session resumption support (--resume flag, check active_session_id)
Create error handling for assessment failures, resource issues, and interruptions
Add feedback collection after each KP (difficulty, resource quality, time spent)
Test with existing profile and plan from .workflow/learn/
Document integration points with profile and plan commands
